name: Potential DLL Hijacking
notes:
This rule triggers when a dynamic-link library (DLL) file is created or downloaded, and loaded by an application. This can indicate a DLL search order hijacking. 

Note: Exclude processes to monitor to reduce false positives
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, Audit.Object Load Success
when the event matches File Extension (custom) is any of dll
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [backgroundTaskHost.exe or cleanmgr.exe]
when at least 2 events are seen with the same Filename (custom) and different Low Level Category in 2 minutes
thresholds: when at least 2 events are seen with the same Filename (custom) and different Low Level Category in 2 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Potential DLL Hijacking on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, Audit.Object Load Success
AND when the event matches File Extension (custom) is any of dll
AND NOT when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [backgroundTaskHost.exe or cleanmgr.exe]
AND when at least 2 events are seen with the same Filename (custom) and different Low Level Category in 2 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Potential DLL Search Order Hijacking
Event description: Detects when a dynamic-link library (DLL) file is created or downloaded, and loaded by an application. This can indicate a DLL search order hijacking.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
